L OANDEPOT. COM, LLC PRIVACY NOTICE — WHAT WE DO WITH YOUR
P ERSONAL INFORMAT ION
Privacy Notice
LOAN 5245838859 / LOANDEPOT.COM, LLC / 02/10/2024
This Privacy Notice describes how loanDepot.com, LLC ("loanDepot.com, LLC," "we," "us," or
"our") collects, shares, and protects the personal information of consumers and customers in
connection with the loan identified as 5245838859 and the related residential financing secured
by the property located at 4357 Baker Underpass Suite 711, West Robinbury, UT 08778.
Financial companies choose how they share your personal information, and federal law gives
consumers the right to limit some but not all sharing. This notice also explains the choices
available to you and how you may exercise them. Please read it carefully and retain a copy with
your loan records. The provisions of this notice apply throughout the life of your account and
after the relationship ends, except as otherwise required or permitted by law.
1. WHY WE PROVIDE THIS NOTICE
Financial companies choose how they share your personal information, and federal law, including the
Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act and its implementing regulations, requires us to tell you how we collect, share,
and protect that information. We are providing this notice so that you understand what we do and the
choices you may have. The types of personal information we collect and share depend on the product or
service you have with us and on the nature of our relationship. We furnish this notice when you become a
customer and, as required by applicable law, on a periodic basis for so long as you maintain a continuing
relationship with loanDepot.com, LLC.
When you are no longer our customer, we continue to share your information as described in this notice,
subject to the limitations imposed by law. Nothing in this notice is intended to expand or restrict any
rights you may have under other agreements between you and loanDepot.com, LLC or under applicable
federal and state law. If state law provides you with additional rights or protections, we will honor those
rights to the extent they apply to your account and to the information we maintain about you.
2. INFORMATION WE COLLECT
We collect personal information about you in connection with providing you a financial product or
service. The information we collect depends on the product or service you request and may include your
name, address, and telephone number; your Social Security number and date of birth; your income,
employment information, and assets; and your account balances, payment history, and transaction or
loss history. We obtain much of this information directly from you on applications and other forms, and
through your communications with us by telephone, in writing, or electronically.
We also collect your personal information from others, such as credit bureaus, affiliates, and other
companies. For example, we may receive your credit history, creditworthiness, and credit scores from
Retain this document with your loan file records.

consumer reporting agencies, and we may receive verification of employment, deposits, and other
account information from third parties you authorize. We maintain records relating to the origination,
servicing, and, where applicable, the transfer of your loan, including documents you sign at closing and
information generated during the life of your account.
The categories of information we collect and the manner in which we use that information may change
over time as products, services, and legal requirements evolve. Where required, we will update this
notice to reflect material changes in our information practices. We collect only the personal information
reasonably necessary to provide the products and services you request, to administer your account, and
to comply with our legal and regulatory obligations.
3. HOW WE SHARE YOUR INFORMATION
All financial companies need to share customers' personal information to run their everyday business. In
the section that follows, we describe the reasons financial companies can share their customers'
personal information, the reasons loanDepot.com, LLC chooses to share, and whether you can limit that
sharing. Whether you can limit sharing depends on the reason for which the information is shared and on
the protections afforded to you by federal and state law.
We may share your personal information for our everyday business purposes, such as to process your
transactions, maintain your account, respond to court orders and legal investigations, or report to credit
bureaus. We may also share for our own marketing purposes, to offer our products and services to you,
and for joint marketing with other financial companies with which we have formal agreements. For these
reasons, sharing may occur without your ability to limit it, except where federal or state law provides
otherwise.
We may further share your personal information for purposes related to our affiliates' everyday business,
including information about your transactions and experiences and, in certain circumstances, information
about your creditworthiness. Sharing for nonaffiliates to market to you, and certain sharing of credit
information among affiliates, are categories of sharing that you may have the right to limit, as more fully
described in the sections of this notice addressing affiliates, nonaffiliates, and your choices.
4. REASONS YOU CANNOT LIMIT SHARING
There are certain everyday business purposes for which federal law does not permit you to limit our
sharing of your personal information. These include sharing necessary to process your transactions, to
maintain and service your account, to respond to court orders, subpoenas, and legal investigations, and
to report to credit bureaus and other consumer reporting agencies. We also share as necessary to
protect against or prevent actual or potential fraud, unauthorized transactions, claims, or other liability,
and to comply with federal, state, and local laws and regulatory requirements.
In addition, we may disclose your personal information to companies that perform services on our behalf,
including providers of document preparation, data processing, account servicing, mailing, collection, and
similar functions. Where we engage such service providers, we require by contract that they maintain the
confidentiality and security of your information and use it only for the limited purposes for which it was
disclosed. These disclosures are part of running our everyday business and are not subject to your right
to limit sharing under applicable law.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

5. SHARING WITH AFFILIATES
Affiliates are companies related to us by common ownership or control, and they can include both
financial and nonfinancial companies. We may share with our affiliates information about your
transactions and experiences with us, such as your account history and payment record. This type of
information sharing is permitted by law and is generally not subject to your right to opt out, although we
will honor any additional limitations required under applicable state law.
We may also share among our affiliates certain other information about you, including information we
obtain from your application and from consumer reporting agencies relating to your creditworthiness.
Federal law gives you the right to limit the sharing of this category of information so that our affiliates
may not use it to market their products and services to you. If you wish to limit such sharing, you may do
so by following the instructions set out in the section of this notice describing your choices and how to
contact us.
Any election you make to limit affiliate sharing will apply to the joint account holders associated with loan
5245838859 to the extent permitted by law, unless we are instructed otherwise. Your election will remain
in effect until you notify us of a change, and we will apply your choice within a reasonable period after we
receive and process your request.
6. SHARING WITH NONAFFILIATES
Nonaffiliates are companies not related to us by common ownership or control, and they can also include
both financial and nonfinancial companies. loanDepot.com, LLC does not share your personal
information with nonaffiliates so that they can market their own products and services to you, except as
permitted or required by law. Where the law would otherwise permit such sharing, we provide you the
opportunity to limit it as described in the section of this notice addressing your choices.
We may, however, disclose your personal information to nonaffiliates in order to carry out our everyday
business purposes, including to investors and purchasers in connection with the sale, transfer,
securitization, or servicing of your loan, and to service providers acting on our behalf. We may also
disclose information to nonaffiliates as necessary to complete or service the transactions you request, to
comply with legal process, and to protect our rights and property. Such disclosures are made consistent
with applicable law and the confidentiality safeguards described in this notice.
7. JOINT MARKETING ARRANGEMENTS
A joint marketing arrangement is a formal agreement between nonaffiliated financial companies that
together market financial products or services to you. loanDepot.com, LLC may share your personal
information with other financial companies with which we have such agreements in order to jointly offer
financial products or services that may be of interest to you. We enter into joint marketing arrangements
only with companies that agree by contract to use the information solely for the purposes of the joint
marketing and to protect the confidentiality and security of your information.
Federal law does not give you the right to limit sharing for joint marketing of financial products and
services, although you may have the right under applicable law to opt out of receiving certain marketing
communications. If you prefer not to receive marketing offers resulting from a joint marketing
Retain this document with your loan file records.

arrangement, you may contact us using the information provided at the end of this notice, and we will
take reasonable steps to honor your preference within the time permitted by law.
8. YOUR CHOICES AND HOW TO LIMIT SHARING
If you are a new customer, we may begin sharing your information for the purposes described in this
notice on the day we send it to you. When you are no longer our customer, we continue to share your
information as described in this notice, subject to applicable law. You may, however, contact us at any
time to limit the categories of sharing for which the law gives you a right to opt out, including the sharing
of certain credit information among our affiliates for their marketing purposes and, where applicable,
sharing with nonaffiliates so that they may market to you.

9. HOW WE PROTECT YOUR INFORMATION
To protect your personal information from unauthorized access and use, we maintain physical, electronic,
and procedural safeguards that comply with applicable federal and state standards. These measures
include computer safeguards, secured files and buildings, access controls, and employee training
designed to limit access to your information to those who need it to provide products and services to

technology and risks change.
We restrict access to your nonpublic personal information to employees, agents, and service providers
who have a legitimate business need for it, and we require those parties to maintain the confidentiality
and security of the information. When we engage third parties to perform services on our behalf, we

and we encourage you to protect your account credentials and to notify us promptly of any suspected
unauthorized activity.
10. INFORMATION FOR FORMER CUSTOMERS
Even after you cease to be a customer of loanDepot.com, LLC, we continue to maintain and protect your

This Equal Credit Opportunity Act Notice is furnished to Vicente Lugo, LLC in connection with loan number 123456789 and the credit application you recently submitted at 557 East Charleston St., Las Vegas, NV 89104. The federal Equal Credit Opportunity Act prohibits creditors from discriminating against credit applicants on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age (provided the applicant has the capacity to enter into a binding contract), because all or part of the applicant's income derives from any public assistance program, or because the applicant has in good faith exercised any right under the Consumer Credit Protection Act. This notice is provided to you pursuant to the requirements of this law, effective as of February 18, 2014. 1. PROHIBITED BASSIS OF DISCRIMINATION The Equal Credit Opportunity Act prohibits lenders, including InHouseLoans, LLC, from discriminating against an applicant with respect to any aspect of a credit transaction. This means that every credit transaction must be judged on its own individual merits, without regard to protected classes of individuals. This prohibition is particularly important when making a determination regarding creditworthiness and whether to approve an application. 2. TREATMENT OF MARITAL STATUS Creditors with respect to InHouseLoans, LLC will refuse to inquire whether you are married or unmarried unless the extension of credit, generally secured by a dwelling, will be made in the name of both spouses. A creditor may consider a spouse's income, or that of a person who will be liable on the account, in connection with applying for credit, only when you expressly permit such consideration. The Federal Equal Credit Opportunity Act prohibits a lender from discriminating against you on the basis of your marital status. 3. NOTIFICATION OF ACTION TAKEN Creditors at InHouseLoans, LLC will notify Vicente Lugo of the action taken on his application for credit within thirty days of the date of the application. If the application is denied, the reasons for the denial will be given on request. If your application is approved and you need credit in a greater amount than what was initially requested, you will be given a written statement of those conditions. 4. RIGHT TO A STATEMENT OF REASONS InHouseLoans, LLC will inform you of the reasons for denying credit, or not extending credit, as applicable, you have the right under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act to a written statement of reasons for the denial within thirty days of the action taken. If you have any questions, please contact Vicente Lugo or InHouseLoans, LLC at 123-456-7890 or visit our office at your convenience.

5. ADVERSE ACTION AND CREDIT SCORING

This information will typically be provided to you in a notice form similar to the one described below.  
This notice describes your rights under the Fair Credit Reporting Act and a summary of your rights:  
You have the right to request a copy of your consumer report from any consumer reporting agency. You also have the right to dispute incomplete or inaccurate information with the consumer reporting agency.  

6. GOVERNMENT MONITORING INFORMATION  
For applications that are intended to be secured by a principal dwelling, LendingPoint.com, LLC is required under Regulation B to request certain information about your ethnicity, race, and sex. The data requested will be used for monitoring purposes in compliance with federal regulations. The request for this data is optional; however, if you do not provide this information, it may limit the ability of LendingPoint.com, LLC to verify the information.  

Discrimination based on the basis of such information is prohibited by law. If you choose not to provide this information, it will not affect your creditworthiness. 

7. APPRAISALS AND VALUATIONS  
An appraisal must be ordered by LendingPoint.com, LLC for a loan if a dwelling is securing the loan. LendingPoint.com, LLC will provide Borrowers with a list of appraisers and it is critical that you understand that to the extent reasonable and responsible, the appraisers will provide appraisals that are based on relevant market data, including the previous sales of comparable properties and other appropriate criteria.

The appraisal will be an independent process to ensure that you receive an accurate valuation for your property. Furthermore, you will be provided with a copy of the appraisal once it is complete.  

If your property is valued lower than expected, it may affect your ability to receive the loan.  

8. INFORMATION REQUESTS AND OPTIONAL DISCLOSURES

You are also entitled to receive approximately the same information that was provided to you in the event of a loan denial, including any reports from consumer reporting agencies that may have aided in making the decision to deny your application.  

This information will be available to you in writing within 30 days of the receipt of your request. However, it is important that you make your request clear to LendingPoint.com, LLC so that they may assist you accordingly.

Contents of the Use of Electronic Records and Signatures

Documenting Standards and Best Practices
1. Purpose

The purpose of this document is to outline the standards and best practices for the use of electronic records and signatures in our organization. This document will provide guidance on the acceptable use and retention of electronic records and signatures, ensuring compliance with relevant regulations.

2. Scope

This document applies to all departments and employees who create, manage, or store electronic records and signatures. It encompasses the processes and tools used to collect, store, and manage electronic records.

3. Definitions

'Electronic Records' refers to records that are created, generated, sent, communicated, or received in electronic form. 'Electronic Signatures' are data in electronic form which is attached to or logically associated with a record and is intended to serve as a signature.

4. Policy

All electronic records must be managed in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, and organizational policies. Electronic signatures must be obtained in a manner that ensures their authenticity and integrity. 

5. Responsibilities

It is the responsibility of department heads to ensure their teams are trained and compliant with this document. Employees are responsible for following the established policies and procedures.

6. Compliance and Audit

Regular audits will be conducted to ensure adherence to the standards outlined in this document. Non-compliance may result in corrective actions.

7. Revision History

This document will be reviewed annually and revised as necessary. Changes and updates will be documented in the revision history section.

NOTICE & DISCLOSURE  
This Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing Rights is provided to Victoria Layos in compliance with loan number: 2345698201 secured by the property located at 4837 East Tinkham St, West Richland, WA 99353, to acknowledge your rights under the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) and other applicable laws regarding the transfer of servicing rights for your mortgage loan. This Notice contains important information, including who is servicing your mortgage loan, what that means for you, and how to obtain further information about your mortgage loan servicing process.  

1. PURPOSE OF THIS NOTICE  
You are receiving this Notice because the servicing of your mortgage loan, that is, the right to receive and process scheduled payments of principal, interest, and any other charges due under your loan, has been transferred from your current servicer to our new servicer as described below.  

2. WHAT SERVICING MEANS  
The term servicing refers to the administration and handling of your mortgage loan after closing. A servicer is the company responsible for collecting your monthly principal and interest payments, managing your escrow account, and responding to customer inquiries.  

If your servicing is transferred, of note, the new servicer facilitates payment processing, the furnishing of the same loan detail over. The borrower is advised to ensure that the new servicer gets all the inherited obligations of the mortgage agreement.  

3. TRANSFER OF YOUR SERVICING  
Effective as of the transfer date described above, the servicing of your mortgage loan will be transferred from [current servicer] to [new servicer] [insert effective transfer date]. The new servicer is responsible for handling your payments and inquiries regarding your loan after the transfer date.  

4. IDENTIFY OF YOUR NEW SERVICER  
Your new servicer is [new servicer name], LLC at [new servicer address]. For information concerning your servicing, your loan details, property listings, and other responsibilities, please contact your new servicer at the following: [contact details for the new servicer].  

5. WHERE TO SEND YOUR PAYMENTS  
All payments should be sent to [new servicer name], LLC which has your prior servicer address. Payments may be remitted to the address for [new servicer] as described in section 4. Payment methods will typically remain unchanged but check should be made for updated policies and procedures regarding payments to [new servicer].

Payments received by your service provider after the standard due date will generally be forwarded to loanscape.com, LLC. You should not rely on such forwarding as a consistent method of payment. To avoid delays, you should send all payments to the address specified by loanscape.com, LLC. If you have any questions about the status of your payment, you should contact loanscape.com, LLC for information on these matters.

6. SIXTY-DAY LATE FEE PROTECTION  
Following a payment from your provider in response to a request for servicing, during the very 60 days prior to requesting the effective date of the notice, you must inform the service provider in writing. If your payment already has been processed, there are no fees involved, and you must continue to adhere to the obligations outlined in your agreement with loanscape.com, LLC. 

7. YOUR CONTINUING PAYMENT OBLIGATION  
The obligation on your part to pay, whether requested, acknowledged, or made in any manner under previous servicing arrangements, continues throughout your period of service. You understand that if payments are not received by the service provider on time, there may be fees associated with this service. 

If you would like to obtain a detailed statement, you should contact loanscape.com, LLC property at 1-877-412-4245 to discuss the particulars regarding your obligations and ongoing requirements. 

8. ESCROW ACCOUNT AND INSURANCE  
If your loan includes an escrow account for the payment of property taxes, homeowner's insurance, mortgage insurance, or other charges, the administration of that account is subject to the terms of your mortgage agreement with loanscape.com, LLC. You acknowledge that payments into the escrow account are subject to change based on variations in estimates for property taxes and insurance premiums that are included in your statements. 

9. NOTICES OF ERROR AND INFORMATION REQUESTS  
If you believe that an error has occurred in your account, you have the right to submit a written notice of error to loanscape.com, LLC. You should describe the error and provide any necessary details to help resolve the issue properly. If you need more information regarding your account, you may submit a request to loanscape.com, LLC as well. 

Upon receiving a qualifying written notice of error, loanscape.com, LLC will provide a written acknowledgment within five days of receipt of your notice. You will be informed if an error has been found, and appropriate corrective measures will be taken.

10. COMMUNICATIONS AND ACCOUNT INFORMATION  
On and after the date hereof, loanscape.com, LLC may provide you with periodic statements, correspondence, and notices regarding your account and related information. If your address changes, you should notify loanscape.com, LLC promptly to ensure you continue to receive necessary documentation. 

You may contact loanscape.com, LLC using the address provided on your statements, or access account information at loanscape.com. If you have any questions, please call loanscape.com, LLC at 1-877-412-4245 for assistance. 

This is a disclosure document and may not be deleted.

Schedule of Fees and Charges

Issued to VIctorIa Lopez DATE 02/10/2024

NOTICE & DISCLOSURE
This Schedule of Fees and Charges is furnished by loanDepot.com, LLC ("Lender," "we," "us," or "our") to Victoria Lopez ("Borrower," "you," or "your") in
connection with the loan identified as 5245838859 and secured by the property located at 4357 Baker Underpass Suite 711, West Robinbury, UT 08778. It
describes the categories of fees, service charges, and reimbursable advances that may be assessed in the servicing of your account under applicable law and the
terms of your note and security instrument. This Schedule is provided for informational purposes, does not modify the loan documents, and should be read
together with them. Fees described here may not all apply to your transaction, and additional charges permitted by law may be assessed as circumstances require.
§ 1. GENERAL FEE TERMS
The fees and charges summarized in this Schedule are imposed in accordance with the terms of your note, your security instrument, and applicable federal and UT law. Not
every fee described will apply to your loan; whether a particular charge is assessed depends on the activity occurring on your account, the services you request, and
conditions affecting the property. Where a fee is authorized by your loan documents but not specifically enumerated here, that authorization continues to govern. We may
assess any fee permitted by law and contract at the time the related service is performed or the related event occurs, and such fees become part of the amount owed on your
account.
Unless otherwise required by law or expressly stated in your loan documents, fees are earned when the corresponding service is rendered and are generally nonrefundable.
Certain fees reflect amounts charged to us by third parties, including governmental authorities, insurers, attorneys, inspectors, and other service providers, and such
amounts may vary by provider, jurisdiction, and the nature of the work performed. We reserve the right to advance these amounts on your behalf and to seek
reimbursement as provided in your security instrument. Any fee collected in excess of the amount permitted by law will be applied or refunded as required by applicable
law.
This Schedule is provided as a convenience and a general guide; it is not an exhaustive list of every charge that may arise during the life of your loan. The absence of a
particular fee from this Schedule does not waive our right to assess it where authorized. To the extent any provision of this Schedule conflicts with your note, your security
instrument, or applicable law, the controlling document or the law governs. We will furnish more specific fee information upon request, subject to any charge for
documents disclosed elsewhere in this Schedule.
§ 2. RETURNED PAYMENT CHARGES
If any payment, instrument, or other tender submitted toward your account is returned, dishonored, reversed, or otherwise not honored by the financial institution on which
it is drawn, for any reason, we may assess a returned-item charge in the maximum amount permitted by applicable UT law and by your loan documents. This charge
applies regardless of the form of the tender, including checks, drafts, electronic funds transfers, and automated clearing house entries, and regardless of whether the
dishonor results from insufficient funds, a closed or frozen account, a stop-payment order, or other cause.
A returned payment does not constitute payment of the installment to which it was directed, and the related installment will remain due and unpaid until satisfied by good
funds. Consequently, a returned payment may also result in the assessment of a late charge if the substitute payment is not received within the applicable grace period, and
may affect the reporting of your account to credit bureaus. Following a returned item, we may, to the extent permitted by law, require that future payments be made by
certified funds, cashier's check, money order, or another guaranteed method for a reasonable period.
The returned-item charge is intended to compensate us for the administrative costs and processing expense associated with handling a dishonored tender and is in addition
to, and not in lieu of, any other remedy available to us. Where applicable law establishes a maximum dollar amount or limits the frequency with which such a charge may
be imposed, we will observe that limit. Returned-item charges, once assessed, are due and payable and may be added to the balance of your account if not paid separately.
§ 3. LATE CHARGES
Your note establishes the date on which each periodic payment is due and the grace period, if any, within which payment may be received without a late charge. If the full
amount of a scheduled payment is not received by the end of the applicable grace period, we may assess a late charge calculated and limited in the manner set forth in your
note and as permitted by applicable UT and federal law. The late charge compensates us for the added expense of handling a delinquent payment and does not represent
interest on the overdue amount.
A late charge is assessed with respect to a delinquent installment and is not affected by the subsequent receipt of that installment; payment of the past-due installment after
the grace period does not relieve the obligation to pay the late charge that has already accrued. Partial payments that do not equal the full amount of a scheduled installment
may not prevent the assessment of a late charge, and we are not obligated to accept a partial payment except as required by your loan documents or applicable law.
Multiple consecutive delinquencies may result in late charges on each affected installment.
Late charges are due when assessed and may be collected from subsequent payments to the extent permitted by the payment-application terms of your loan documents and
applicable law, or added to the unpaid balance of your account. We may, in our discretion and without obligation, waive a late charge in an individual instance; any such
waiver applies only to the specific charge waived and does not establish a course of dealing or waive our right to assess late charges on other delinquent installments.
§ 4. PAYOFF AND DEMAND STATEMENT FEES
Upon request, we will prepare and furnish a statement setting forth the amount required to pay your loan in full as of a specified date, commonly referred to as a payoff
statement, demand statement, or beneficiary statement. Where permitted by applicable UT law and your loan documents, we may charge a reasonable fee for the
preparation and delivery of such a statement, including any fee authorized for delivery by facsimile, expedited mail, or other expedited means. Certain jurisdictions require
that an initial payoff statement be provided without charge or within a prescribed period, and we will comply with any such requirement.
A payoff statement reflects amounts due as of the stated good-through date and is contingent upon the receipt of good funds on or before that date. Because interest, fees,
advances, and other charges may continue to accrue, a payoff statement may include a per-diem interest figure and may expire if payment is not received by the good-
Retain this document with your loan file records.

through date, in which event an updated statement may be required and an additional preparation fee may apply. Amounts shown in a payoff statement remain subject to
final verification, including the clearance of recent payments and the resolution of any pending charges.
We may also charge reasonable fees, to the extent permitted by law, for the preparation and recording of documents necessary to release, reconvey, or satisfy the lien
securing your loan upon payment in full, as well as for any recording, courier, or governmental charge incurred in connection with such release. Fees associated with lien
release and reconveyance, where assessable, are typically disclosed at or before payoff and may be required to be collected as part of the payoff amount in accordance with
applicable UT law.
§ 5. OPTIONAL EXPEDITED PAYMENT SERVICES
As a convenience, we may from time to time offer optional services that allow you to make a payment by telephone, through an online portal, or through a customer
service representative, with same-day or expedited processing. These services are entirely optional. You are never required to use an expedited payment service to make a
timely payment, and you may always submit your payment without charge by the standard methods described in your monthly statement and loan documents, including by
mail and through scheduled automated transfers.
Where we offer an optional expedited payment service for which a fee is charged, the fee, the available processing options, and the cutoff times for same-day crediting will
be disclosed to you before you authorize the transaction, and your authorization to proceed constitutes your agreement to the disclosed fee. Such convenience fees
compensate us for the cost of providing the expedited channel and are separate from, and in addition to, the payment amount applied to your account. We will not impose
an expedited payment fee where prohibited by applicable law.
The availability, terms, and pricing of optional expedited payment services may change, and any such service may be modified, suspended, or discontinued at our
discretion without affecting your obligation to make timely payments by other means. Using an expedited service does not alter the contractual due date or grace period
applicable to your loan; a payment made through an expedited channel is credited in accordance with the payment-application terms of your loan documents and applicable
law as of the date the payment is actually received in good funds.
§ 6. PROPERTY INSPECTION AND PRESERVATION
If your loan becomes delinquent, or if the property securing your loan appears to be vacant, abandoned, or inadequately maintained, we may, to the extent permitted by
your security instrument and applicable UT law, conduct or arrange property inspections to assess the condition and occupancy of the property. The reasonable cost of each
such inspection may be charged to your account. Inspections are performed to protect the value of the property and our interest in it and do not relieve you of your
obligation to maintain the property as required by your loan documents.
Where the property is found to require protective measures, we may incur expenses to preserve and protect it, which may include securing the premises, winterization,
debris removal, repair of conditions that threaten habitability or value, and similar property-preservation activities authorized by your security instrument. The reasonable
costs of such preservation efforts may be advanced by us and charged to your account as amounts secured by the security instrument, bearing interest as and to the extent
provided in your loan documents.
Property inspection and preservation charges reflect amounts billed to us by third-party vendors or governmental authorities and may vary with the location, condition, and
circumstances of the property. We will assess such charges only as permitted by applicable law and your loan documents, and we will not charge for an inspection or
preservation activity more frequently than is reasonably necessary to protect the security for the loan.
§ 7. FORCE-PLACED INSURANCE CHARGES
Your security instrument requires you to maintain hazard, flood, and any other insurance coverage required by us on the property securing your loan. If you fail to maintain
required coverage, or if you do not provide satisfactory evidence of such coverage upon request, we may, after providing any notices required by applicable law, purchase
insurance to protect our interest in the property. This coverage is commonly referred to as force-placed, lender-placed, or creditor-placed insurance, and the cost of such
coverage may be charged to you and added to the amount you owe.
Force-placed insurance is obtained solely to protect our interest in the property and may be substantially more expensive than coverage you could obtain on your own. It
may provide less protection than a policy you select, may not cover your personal property or personal liability, and may not name you as an insured beneficiary. We
strongly encourage you to maintain your own adequate insurance at all times to avoid the placement of, and charges for, lender-placed coverage. Upon receipt of
satisfactory evidence that you maintained acceptable coverage during the relevant period, we will cancel any overlapping force-placed coverage and refund or credit
charges as required by applicable law.
Premiums and related charges for force-placed insurance reflect amounts charged to us by the insurer and applicable governmental charges, and such amounts may be
advanced by us and treated as amounts secured by the security instrument. We will provide the notices, timing, and disclosures required by applicable federal and UT law
before assessing, and in connection with, force-placed insurance charges, and we will comply with all applicable limitations on the imposition of such charges.
§ 8. TAX-RELATED ADVANCES
Your loan documents obligate you to pay, when due, all real property taxes, special assessments, and similar governmental charges levied against the property securing
your loan. If such taxes or assessments become delinquent and are not paid, or if a tax lien or sale threatens our interest in the property, we may, as permitted by your
security instrument and applicable UT law, advance funds to pay the delinquent taxes, assessments, penalties, and related charges in order to protect the security for the
loan. Amounts so advanced may be charged to your account.
Tax-related advances reflect amounts paid by us to taxing authorities and may include penalties, interest, and redemption costs imposed by those authorities, as well as
reasonable service charges for the research and processing necessary to identify and satisfy the obligation. Such advances are treated as amounts secured by the security
instrument and may bear interest as and to the extent provided in your loan documents. Making a tax advance does not waive any default arising from your failure to pay
taxes when due, nor does it obligate us to make any further advance.
If your loan includes an escrow or impound account for the payment of taxes and insurance, the administration of that account, including any shortage, surplus, or
deficiency, is governed by the separate escrow disclosures provided to you and by applicable law, including the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act where applicable.
Tax-related advances described in this section generally arise where there is no escrow account, where escrowed funds are insufficient, or where amounts otherwise remain
unpaid and threaten the priority or security of our lien.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

§ 9. DOCUMENT AND RESEARCH FEES
Upon your request, and to the extent permitted by applicable UT and federal law and your loan documents, we may provide copies of loan documents, account histories,
year-end statements, amortization schedules, and similar records, and we may charge a reasonable fee for the research, reproduction, and delivery of such materials. Certain
documents and disclosures are provided without charge as required by law, including statements and notices that we are obligated to furnish, and no fee will be assessed
where the law prohibits it or requires that the item be provided free of charge.
We may also charge reasonable fees for special services you request that fall outside the ordinary servicing of your account, such as the preparation of subordination
agreements, partial release documents, modification documents, assumption processing, or responses to certain third-party requests, in each case only to the extent the
charge is authorized by your loan documents and applicable law. The amount of any such fee may reflect the complexity of the request and amounts charged to us by third
parties, including attorneys, title companies, and recording offices.
Where a requested service requires payment to a governmental authority or other third party, such as a recording fee or a charge for a title or lien search, the actual amount
charged to us may be passed through to you in addition to any service fee. We will, upon request and where reasonably practicable, advise you of an applicable document
or research fee before performing the service so that you may decide whether to proceed.
§ 10. DEFAULT AND COLLECTION COSTS
In the event of a default under your note or security instrument, you may, to the extent permitted by those documents and applicable UT and federal law, be responsible for
the reasonable costs and expenses we incur in enforcing our rights and protecting our interest in the property. Such costs may include, without limitation, attorneys' fees,
court costs, trustee's fees, title and lien search charges, foreclosure and bankruptcy-related fees, and other expenses authorized by your loan documents and permitted by
law. These amounts may be added to the balance of your account as provided in your security instrument.
Default-related charges reflect amounts billed to us by attorneys, trustees, courts, and other third parties, and the nature and amount of such charges depend on the stage of
any enforcement proceeding, the jurisdiction in which the property is located, and the requirements of applicable law. We will assess such charges only to the extent they
are actually incurred, are reasonable, and are permitted by your loan documents and applicable law, and we will observe any statutory limitations, notice requirements, and
reinstatement rights that apply.
Curing a default by bringing your account current, where a right to do so exists, may require payment of accrued default and collection costs in addition to the past-due
amounts, to the extent permitted by law and your loan documents. Nothing in this section creates an obligation on our part to accept a partial cure or to forbear from
exercising any remedy, except as required by applicable law or by the terms of your loan documents.
§ 11. HOW FEES MAY CHANGE
The fees and charges described in this Schedule may change from time to time. Many of the amounts reflect charges imposed on us by third parties or by governmental
authorities, which we do not control and which may increase or decrease without notice to us. Other fees may be adjusted to reflect changes in our costs, changes in
applicable law, or changes in the maximum amounts permitted by your loan documents and by applicable UT and federal law. Where a fee is set by a controlling statute or
regulation, the amount we may charge will rise or fall with that legal limit.
We will provide advance notice of changes to fees where notice is required by applicable law or by your loan documents, and any such change will take effect only as
permitted by law and the governing documents. The continued servicing of your account, and your continued use of optional services for which a fee is disclosed, are
subject to the fees in effect at the time the related service is performed or the related event occurs. This Schedule may be amended or superseded; the most recent version
made available to you, together with your loan documents and applicable law, controls.
Nothing in this Schedule obligates us to charge any particular fee, and our decision not to assess a fee on one occasion does not waive our right to assess that fee, or any
other fee, on a later occasion. If you have questions about a specific charge on your account, or about whether a fee applies to your loan, you may contact us at
+1-857-816-4859 or in writing at 1575 Elizabeth Tunnel Suite 900, North Thomasside, AL 58337, and we will respond as required by applicable law.
§ 12. WAIVERS AND DISCRETION
We may, in our sole discretion and without obligation, reduce or waive a particular fee or charge in an individual case, including as an accommodation in connection with a
hardship, a payment arrangement, or a loss-mitigation effort. Any such reduction or waiver applies only to the specific charge addressed and only for the occasion on which
it is granted. A waiver of one fee is not a waiver of any other fee, is not a modification of your loan documents or this Schedule, and does not establish a custom, practice,
or course of dealing upon which you may rely with respect to future charges.
No waiver of any fee, charge, right, or remedy is effective unless and only to the extent we grant it, and our acceptance of a late or partial payment, our forbearance in
collecting a charge, or our delay in exercising a right does not constitute a waiver of that charge or right or of our ability to enforce the full terms of your loan documents.
To the extent applicable law requires that a waiver be in writing or follow a particular procedure, we will observe that requirement.
If you believe a fee has been assessed in error, or if you wish to request the waiver or review of a charge, you may contact us at +1-857-816-4859 or submit your request in
writing to 1575 Elizabeth Tunnel Suite 900, North Thomasside, AL 58337, referencing loan 5245838859. We will review timely and properly directed inquiries and will
respond within the period and in the manner required by applicable law, correcting any error and refunding or crediting any amount as required.

Binding Arbitration Agreement

This Binding Arbitration Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into between Victoria Lopez ("you"
or "Borrower") and loanDepot.com, LLC ("we," "us," or "Lender") in connection with the loan
identified as loan number 5245838859 and any account, transaction, or relationship arising
therefrom. By signing the related loan documents or otherwise accepting the terms set forth
herein, you acknowledge that you have read this Agreement, that you understand it materially
affects your legal rights, and that you agree to resolve Covered Disputes through individual
binding arbitration rather than in court before a judge or jury. Please read this Agreement
carefully and retain a copy for your records. Capitalized terms not defined herein have the
meanings given in the loan documents.
1. AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE
You and loanDepot.com, LLC agree that, except as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement, any
Covered Dispute (as defined below) shall be resolved by neutral, binding arbitration administered on an
individual basis, and not by a court, jury, or any form of class, collective, representative, or consolidated
proceeding. This Agreement reflects the mutual intention of the parties to provide a fair, efficient, and
confidential forum for the resolution of disputes. By agreeing to arbitrate, both you and the Lender
knowingly and voluntarily relinquish certain rights that would otherwise be available in a judicial forum, as
further described in the sections that follow.
This Agreement is governed by the Federal Arbitration Act, 9 U.S.C. Section 1 et seq. (the "FAA"), and
evidences a transaction involving interstate commerce. To the extent the FAA is held inapplicable to any
issue, the arbitration laws of the UT in which the property securing the loan, located at 4357 Baker
Underpass Suite 711, West Robinbury, UT 08778, is situated shall apply, provided that such laws do not
conflict with the FAA. The parties intend that this Agreement be interpreted broadly to give effect to their
agreement to arbitrate the widest range of disputes permitted by applicable law.
This Agreement applies regardless of whether the Covered Dispute arises before, during, or after the
term of the loan, and regardless of whether the loan or the underlying obligation has been paid, satisfied,
charged off, sold, assigned, or otherwise transferred. Your obligations and the Lender's obligations under
this Agreement continue notwithstanding the closing, servicing transfer, or termination of any account or
relationship between the parties.
2. DISPUTES COVERED
A "Covered Dispute" means any claim, dispute, or controversy of any kind, whether based in contract,
tort, statute, regulation, common law, equity, or otherwise, that arises out of or relates in any way to the
Retain this document with your loan file records.

loan, the loan documents, this Agreement, the property securing the loan, the origination, underwriting,
processing, closing, funding, servicing, collection, or enforcement of the loan, or any communication,
disclosure, advertisement, product, or service associated with the relationship between you and
loanDepot.com, LLC. The term is to be construed in the broadest manner permitted by law.
Covered Disputes also include, without limitation, claims relating to the validity, enforceability, scope, or
interpretation of this Agreement, except as expressly reserved for judicial determination herein; claims
regarding alleged fraud, misrepresentation, breach of fiduciary duty, or unfair or deceptive acts or
practices; claims under federal or state consumer-protection, lending, privacy, or fair-credit-reporting
statutes; and claims arising from any prior loan, account, or transaction between you and the Lender.
Claims by or against the Lender's affiliates, agents, employees, officers, directors, successors, assigns,
and servicers are likewise included.
The fact that a Covered Dispute may also constitute a claim by or against a third party shall not defeat
the obligation to arbitrate as between you and loanDepot.com, LLC. If any portion of a dispute falls
within the scope of this Agreement and any portion does not, the parties shall arbitrate the portion that is
covered and may stay the remainder pending the outcome of the arbitration, to the extent permitted by
applicable law and the rules of the administering body.
3. DISPUTES EXCLUDED
Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement does not require arbitration of the following matters: any
action by loanDepot.com, LLC to foreclose upon, or to obtain possession of, the property securing the
loan through judicial or non-judicial foreclosure, power of sale, deed in lieu, ejectment, or similar remedy
authorized under the loan documents or applicable UT law; the exercise of any self-help remedy,
including set-off; or the filing or enforcement of any lien against the collateral. The Lender's pursuit of
such remedies shall not waive its right to compel arbitration of any other Covered Dispute.
This Agreement also does not preclude either party from seeking provisional or ancillary remedies,
including temporary restraining orders, preliminary injunctions, attachment, receivership, or other interim
relief from a court of competent jurisdiction in aid of arbitration or to preserve the status quo pending
arbitration. Seeking or obtaining such relief shall not constitute a waiver of the right or obligation to
arbitrate, and any merits determination of the underlying Covered Dispute shall remain subject to
arbitration.
Nothing in this Agreement limits your right to file a complaint with, or to provide information to, any
federal, state, or local governmental agency or regulatory authority, including any banking or consumer-
protection agency, and to participate in any investigation or proceeding conducted by such authority.
This Agreement does not preclude such an authority from exercising its enforcement powers, nor does it
require you to arbitrate any claim that applicable law expressly provides may not be subjected to pre-
dispute binding arbitration.
4. SMALL-CLAIMS CARVE-OUT
Either you or loanDepot.com, LLC may elect to bring and pursue an individual claim in a small-claims
court of competent jurisdiction in lieu of arbitration, provided that the claim qualifies for that court under
its jurisdictional limits and remains in that forum on an individual basis. This carve-out is intended to
Retain this document with your loan file records.

preserve a simple, low-cost venue for the resolution of modest individual disputes without the
procedures associated with formal arbitration or litigation.
If a claim filed in small-claims court is transferred, removed, appealed to, or otherwise proceeds in a
court other than a small-claims court, or if the claim is amended to seek relief or to assert allegations that
exceed the jurisdiction of the small-claims court, then either party may thereafter elect to compel
arbitration of that claim under this Agreement. Any such election shall be honored by the parties, and the
dispute shall proceed in arbitration in accordance with the terms set forth herein.
A claim brought in small-claims court must be asserted only in the claimant's individual capacity and
may not be maintained as, or consolidated with, any class, collective, or representative action. The class-
action waiver and other individual-relief provisions of this Agreement apply with equal force to any matter
pursued under this small-claims carve-out.
5. ADMINISTERING BODY AND RULES
Unless the parties agree in writing to a different forum, a Covered Dispute submitted to arbitration shall
be administered by a nationally recognized, neutral arbitration administrator that maintains established
procedures for the resolution of consumer disputes, in accordance with that administrator's consumer
arbitration rules in effect at the time the arbitration is commenced. The arbitration shall be conducted by
a single neutral arbitrator who is a retired judge or an attorney experienced in consumer-finance or real-
property matters, selected in accordance with the administrator's rules.
To the extent the chosen administrator's rules conflict with this Agreement, the terms of this Agreement
shall control to the extent permitted by the administrator and applicable law. If the designated
administrator is unavailable or unwilling to administer the arbitration consistent with this Agreement, the
parties shall jointly select a comparable neutral administrator, and failing agreement, a court of
competent jurisdiction may appoint the administrator or arbitrator upon the petition of either party, as
authorized by the FAA.
The arbitrator shall have the authority to determine the arbitrability of any Covered Dispute and to resolve
any dispute concerning the interpretation, applicability, or enforceability of this Agreement, except that
the enforceability of the class-action and representative-action waiver set forth in this Agreement shall be
determined exclusively by a court and not by the arbitrator. The arbitrator shall apply the substantive law
that a court would apply and shall honor applicable statutes of limitation and claims of privilege. You may
obtain the administrator's rules and forms by contacting loanDepot.com, LLC at +1-857-816-4859 or in
writing at 1575 Elizabeth Tunnel Suite 900, North Thomasside, AL 58337.
6. EFFECT OF ARBITRATION; JURY-TRIAL WAIVER
BY AGREEING TO ARBITRATE COVERED DISPUTES, YOU AND LOANDEPOT.COM, LLC EACH
KNOWINGLY, VOLUNTARILY, AND INTENTIONALLY WAIVE THE RIGHT TO A TRIAL BY JURY AND THE
RIGHT TO HAVE COVERED DISPUTES DECIDED BY A JUDGE IN A COURT OF LAW. Arbitration
substitutes a private dispute-resolution process for the procedures available in court. The parties
acknowledge that this waiver is a material inducement to entering into the loan transaction and that each
has had the opportunity to consult with legal counsel of its own choosing regarding the consequences of
this waiver.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

In arbitration, the proceedings are generally more streamlined than litigation, discovery may be more
limited, and the rights to pre-trial and appellate review are substantially narrower than those available in
court. The arbitrator's decision is final and binding, subject only to the limited grounds for review
provided under the FAA. Judgment on the award rendered by the arbitrator may be entered in any court
of competent jurisdiction. The parties accept these features of arbitration in exchange for its efficiency,
finality, and reduced cost.
The arbitrator may award any relief, including monetary damages, declaratory relief, injunctive relief, and
statutory remedies, that a court could award to an individual party under applicable law, and only in favor
of the individual party seeking relief and only to the extent necessary to provide relief warranted by that
party's individual claim. The arbitrator shall issue a reasoned written award setting forth the essential
findings and conclusions on which the award is based, in accordance with the administrator's rules.
7. CLASS-ACTION AND REPRESENTATIVE WAIVER
COVERED DISPUTES SHALL BE ARBITRATED ONLY ON AN INDIVIDUAL BASIS. YOU AND
LOANDEPOT.COM, LLC EACH WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO ARBITRATE OR LITIGATE ANY COVERED
DISPUTE AS A CLASS ACTION, COLLECTIVE ACTION, PRIVATE ATTORNEY-GENERAL ACTION, OR
OTHER REPRESENTATIVE PROCEEDING, OR TO SERVE AS A CLASS REPRESENTATIVE OR CLASS
MEMBER, OR TO PARTICIPATE AS A MEMBER OF A CLASS OF CLAIMANTS. The arbitrator shall have
no authority to consolidate or join the claims of more than one person and may not preside over any form
of class, collective, or representative proceeding.
The parties agree that this class-action and representative waiver is a fundamental and non-severable
element of their agreement to arbitrate. If a court or arbitrator determines that this waiver is
unenforceable or invalid as to a particular Covered Dispute or claim for relief, then, to that extent, that
particular Covered Dispute or claim shall proceed in a court of competent jurisdiction rather than in
arbitration, but all other Covered Disputes and claims shall remain subject to arbitration on an individual
basis as provided in this Agreement.
No arbitration under this Agreement shall be joined or consolidated with any other arbitration involving a
different borrower or party without the written consent of all parties to all affected arbitrations. The
prohibition on class, collective, and representative proceedings applies regardless of the forum and
survives any determination that another provision of this Agreement is unenforceable.
8. COSTS AND FEES
The party initiating an arbitration shall pay the filing fee required by the administrator, except that, upon
your written request, loanDepot.com, LLC will advance or reimburse all or a portion of the filing,
administrative, and arbitrator fees attributable to a Covered Dispute that you initiate, to the extent
provided by the administrator's consumer rules or as otherwise required to ensure that the cost of
arbitration is not prohibitive relative to the cost of pursuing the claim in court. Such advancement does
not waive any right to seek recovery of those amounts in the award.
Each party shall otherwise bear its own attorneys' fees, expert fees, and other costs incurred in
connection with the arbitration, except that the arbitrator may award attorneys' fees and costs to the
prevailing party to the extent such an award would be available under the applicable substantive law

governing the Covered Dispute, or as otherwise provided by the loan documents. The allocation of fees
and costs shall be set forth in the arbitrator's award and is subject to the same finality as the remainder
of the award.
If the arbitrator determines that a claim or defense was frivolous, asserted in bad faith, or brought for an
improper purpose, the arbitrator may reallocate the fees and costs of the arbitration as authorized by the
administrator's rules and applicable law. Nothing in this section limits any statutory right you may have to
recover fees and costs that you would be entitled to recover had the Covered Dispute been adjudicated
in court.
9. LOCATION AND CONDUCT OF ARBITRATION
Unless the parties agree otherwise in writing, any in-person arbitration hearing shall be held in the federal
judicial district that includes your residence or, where applicable, the UT county in which the property
located at 4357 Baker Underpass Suite 711, West Robinbury, UT 08778 is situated. The arbitrator may,
consistent with the administrator's rules, conduct proceedings by telephone, videoconference, or on the
basis of written submissions where appropriate, taking into account the amount in controversy, the
convenience of the parties and witnesses, and the interest in minimizing the cost and burden of the
proceeding.
The arbitration shall be conducted in the English language. The arbitrator shall manage the exchange of
information in a manner proportionate to the dispute, permitting reasonable discovery consistent with the
desire to make arbitration efficient and economical while affording each party a fair opportunity to
present its case. The arbitrator shall have the power to issue subpoenas, rule on the admissibility of
evidence, and enter protective orders as appropriate, in accordance with the administrator's rules and
applicable law.
Except as may be required by law, to enforce or challenge the award, or to seek interim relief, the parties
shall maintain the confidentiality of the arbitration proceedings, including the existence, content, and
result of the arbitration, and shall not disclose the same except to the parties' respective attorneys,
accountants, auditors, insurers, and other advisors who have a need to know and who agree to be
bound by this confidentiality obligation.
10. RIGHT TO OPT OUT
You have the right to reject this Agreement and to opt out of arbitration without affecting any other term
of the loan or your eligibility for credit. To opt out, you must send loanDepot.com, LLC written notice that
you reject this Agreement, stating your name, the property address located at 4357 Baker Underpass

used as a basis to deny, condition, or revoke credit, to alter the terms of your loan, or to retaliate against

Except as otherwise provided with respect to the class-action and representative waiver in Section 7, if

This Agreement shall survive the repayment, prepayment, refinancing, modification, satisfaction, charge-

binding upon the parties and their respective heirs, successors, assigns, and permitted transferees.
This Agreement, together with the arbitration-related provisions of the loan documents, constitutes the

LOANDEPOT.COM, LLC — SCHEDULE OF FEES AND CHARGES
Schedule of Fees and Charges
Issued to VIctorIa Lopez

LENDER loanDepot.com, LLC
LOAN NO. 5245838859
DATE 02/10/2024
BORROWER Victoria Lopez

This Schedule of Fees and Charges is furnished by loanDepot.com, LLC ("Lender," "we," "us," or "our") to Victoria Lopez ("Borrower," "you," or "your") in
connection with the loan identified as 5245838859 and secured by the property located at 4357 Baker Underpass Suite 711, West Robinbury, UT 08778. It
describes the categories of fees, service charges, and reimbursable advances that may be assessed in the servicing of your account under applicable law and the
terms of your note and security instrument. This Schedule is provided for informational purposes, does not modify the loan documents, and should be read
together with them. Fees described here may not all apply to your transaction, and additional charges permitted by law may be assessed as circumstances require.
§ 1. GENERAL FEE TERMS
The fees and charges summarized in this Schedule are imposed in accordance with the terms of your note, your security instrument, and applicable federal and UT law. Not
every fee described will apply to your loan; whether a particular charge is assessed depends on the activity occurring on your account, the services you request, and
conditions affecting the property. Where a fee is authorized by your loan documents but not specifically enumerated here, that authorization continues to govern. We may
assess any fee permitted by law and contract at the time the related service is performed or the related event occurs, and such fees become part of the amount owed on your
account.
Unless otherwise required by law or expressly stated in your loan documents, fees are earned when the corresponding service is rendered and are generally nonrefundable.
Certain fees reflect amounts charged to us by third parties, including governmental authorities, insurers, attorneys, inspectors, and other service providers, and such
amounts may vary by provider, jurisdiction, and the nature of the work performed. We reserve the right to advance these amounts on your behalf and to seek
reimbursement as provided in your security instrument. Any fee collected in excess of the amount permitted by law will be applied or refunded as required by applicable
law.
This Schedule is provided as a convenience and a general guide; it is not an exhaustive list of every charge that may arise during the life of your loan. The absence of a
particular fee from this Schedule does not waive our right to assess it where authorized. To the extent any provision of this Schedule conflicts with your note, your security
instrument, or applicable law, the controlling document or the law governs. We will furnish more specific fee information upon request, subject to any charge for
documents disclosed elsewhere in this Schedule.
§ 2. RETURNED PAYMENT CHARGES
If any payment, instrument, or other tender submitted toward your account is returned, dishonored, reversed, or otherwise not honored by the financial institution on which
it is drawn, for any reason, we may assess a returned-item charge in the maximum amount permitted by applicable UT law and by your loan documents. This charge
applies regardless of the form of the tender, including checks, drafts, electronic funds transfers, and automated clearing house entries, and regardless of whether the
dishonor results from insufficient funds, a closed or frozen account, a stop-payment order, or other cause.
A returned payment does not constitute payment of the installment to which it was directed, and the related installment will remain due and unpaid until satisfied by good
funds. Consequently, a returned payment may also result in the assessment of a late charge if the substitute payment is not received within the applicable grace period, and
may affect the reporting of your account to credit bureaus. Following a returned item, we may, to the extent permitted by law, require that future payments be made by
certified funds, cashier's check, money order, or another guaranteed method for a reasonable period.
The returned-item charge is intended to compensate us for the administrative costs and processing expense associated with handling a dishonored tender and is in addition
to, and not in lieu of, any other remedy available to us. Where applicable law establishes a maximum dollar amount or limits the frequency with which such a charge may
be imposed, we will observe that limit. Returned-item charges, once assessed, are due and payable and may be added to the balance of your account if not paid separately.
§ 3. LATE CHARGES
Your note establishes the date on which each periodic payment is due and the grace period, if any, within which payment may be received without a late charge. If the full
amount of a scheduled payment is not received by the end of the applicable grace period, we may assess a late charge calculated and limited in the manner set forth in your
note and as permitted by applicable UT and federal law. The late charge compensates us for the added expense of handling a delinquent payment and does not represent
interest on the overdue amount.
A late charge is assessed with respect to a delinquent installment and is not affected by the subsequent receipt of that installment; payment of the past-due installment after
the grace period does not relieve the obligation to pay the late charge that has already accrued. Partial payments that do not equal the full amount of a scheduled installment
may not prevent the assessment of a late charge, and we are not obligated to accept a partial payment except as required by your loan documents or applicable law.
Multiple consecutive delinquencies may result in late charges on each affected installment.
Late charges are due when assessed and may be collected from subsequent payments to the extent permitted by the payment-application terms of your loan documents and
applicable law, or added to the unpaid balance of your account. We may, in our discretion and without obligation, waive a late charge in an individual instance; any such
waiver applies only to the specific charge waived and does not establish a course of dealing or waive our right to assess late charges on other delinquent installments.
§ 4. PAYOFF AND DEMAND STATEMENT FEES
Upon request, we will prepare and furnish a statement setting forth the amount required to pay your loan in full as of a specified date, commonly referred to as a payoff
statement, demand statement, or beneficiary statement. Where permitted by applicable UT law and your loan documents, we may charge a reasonable fee for the
preparation and delivery of such a statement, including any fee authorized for delivery by facsimile, expedited mail, or other expedited means. Certain jurisdictions require
that an initial payoff statement be provided without charge or within a prescribed period, and we will comply with any such requirement.
A payoff statement reflects amounts due as of the stated good-through date and is contingent upon the receipt of good funds on or before that date. Because interest, fees,
advances, and other charges may continue to accrue, a payoff statement may include a per-diem interest figure and may expire if payment is not received by the good-
